text:
PARIS —Bad news for Hezly Rivera’s new legion of fans: The 16-year-old gymnast from Oradell is not expected to compete in Tuesday’s team final, according to atweet from USA Gymnasticsout in advance of the much-anticipated competition.

It is still possible that Rivera could step in for another competitor on the balance beam or the uneven bars, her two strengths. That could depend on the health of U.S. star Simone Biles, who was nursing a calf injury during Sunday’s qualification round.

For now, though, this is the lineup:

Vault:Jordan Chiles, Jade Carey, Biles

Uneven bars:Chiles, Biles, Suni Lee

Balance beam:Chiles, Lee, Biles

Floor exercise:Lee, Chiles, Biles

No matter what happens, though, Rivera will get a medal if the heavily favored American team finishes in the top three on Tuesday at Bercy Arena. Rivera competed in the qualifying portion of the team competition in both the bars and the beam.

Rivera is the youngest of the nearly 600 athletes in Paris as part of Team USA. She was a surprise inclusion on the U.S. team after a series of injuries knocked out more seasoned competitors, but is expected to be a big part of the Americans’ future in international competition.

The team competition begins at 12:15 p.m. ET.
